author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Added 2-D and dynamic-rank interop for Arrow and Polars. Two complementary mappings per backend.

Arrow (arrow_conv.rs):
- array2_to_arrow_columns(a) -> Vec<PrimitiveArray> — one Arrow column per column of the ferray array. This is the natural shape for a downstream Arrow consumer that treats each column as a field.
- array2_from_arrow_columns(cols) -> Array2 — stacks columns back, validating shape/dtype/null-count per column.
- arrayd_to_arrow_flat(a) -> PrimitiveArray — flattens any ND ferray array to a single 1-D Arrow buffer. Round-trip requires the caller to remember the shape.
- arrayd_from_arrow_flat(arr, shape) -> ArrayD — reshape a flat Arrow buffer into a target shape with bounds checking.

Polars (polars_conv.rs):
- array2_to_polars_dataframe(a, column_names) -> DataFrame — one Series per column of the 2-D array. The natural fit for pipelines that hand a DataFrame to further processing.
- array2_from_polars_dataframe(df) -> Array2 — stack the columns, requiring consistent length/dtype and no nulls.

10 new tests across both backends: Array2 roundtrip for f64 and i32, arrayd flat roundtrip incl. reshape-to-different-rank, empty DataFrame/columns, inconsistent column length, wrong dtype, wrong column-name count — plus bound checks on the flat reshape path. All 54 lib tests pass with the arrow and polars features enabled (up from 44). Clippy clean. Arrow/Polars mixed-dtype or null-aware DataFrames are still out of scope; callers with those need to cast/drop-null on the pandas side before handing to ferray.
